data.description:
## Why
R5 changes the review_kernel verification vocabulary, which is SHARED with the code-review gate. The epic's gate row demands proof that code-review decisions are untouched before any kernel change lands - a silent cross-gate regression is the worst failure mode of this epic.

## What
Cross-gate non-regression replay: run the R5-amended kernel (asserted_capability_confirmed sub-answer added) over the existing code-review adjudication corpus (docs/experiments/code_review_adjudication.jsonl) and require BYTE-IDENTICAL decisions: every finding's (decision, priority, validity) unchanged, and the new sub-answer reads na everywhere outside the G6/E4/T3 cohort. Deterministic replay of pass-2/3 math over recorded verifications - no live LLM needed for the decision comparison; a small live sanity slice (~20 findings) confirms the amended prompt still parses.

## Scope
- docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ (replay driver + runs/ diff output + writeup).
- Reads src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (candidate branch build); no packaged changes land from this story.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The replay runs the amended kernel over the full code_review_adjudication.jsonl corpus and commits a decision diff artifact.
- [ ] The diff is EMPTY: byte-identical (decision, priority, validity) for every code-review finding; any non-empty diff fails the gate and is recorded as an R5 blocker.
- [ ] The new sub-answer is na for every finding outside the G6/E4/T3 cohort, asserted by the replay artifact.
- [ ] A docs/experiments/ writeup records method and outcome (the input to R5's gate row).
data.title: E5: cross-gate non-regression replay (review_kernel change vs code-review corpus)
